breaking_news.title: North Korean group UNC1069 compromises Axios npm package in massive supply chain attack
breaking_news.summary:
Attackers compromised the npm account of the Axios library maintainer to publish malicious versions containing a cross-platform remote access trojan. This supply chain attack targeted the widely used HTTP client library, which sees over 100 million weekly downloads across frontend and backend frameworks.

Google Threat Intelligence Group attributed the campaign to UNC1069, a North Korea-nexus threat actor, marking it as one of the most impactful npm attacks on record. Developers who installed the rogue versions before the takedown face significant risk of system compromise and data theft.

breaking_news.title: F5 BIG-IP vulnerability reclassified as critical RCE and actively exploited in the wild
breaking_news.summary:
A vulnerability in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager, previously classified as a denial-of-service issue, has been reclassified as a critical remote code execution flaw. CISA added CVE-2025-53521 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog after confirming active exploitation in the wild.

Hackers are using this flaw to deploy persistent malware with root privileges on unpatched systems, affecting enterprises and government agencies relying on F5 for secure access. The severity score was raised to 9.8, prompting urgent patching requirements for organizations using the affected appliances.

breaking_news.title: CISA orders federal agencies to patch actively exploited Citrix NetScaler vulnerability
breaking_news.summary:
CISA ordered federal agencies to patch a critical memory overread vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler appliances by Thursday due to active exploitation. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-3055, allows unauthenticated attackers to leak sensitive data and obtain administrative session IDs.

This vulnerability impacts Citrix ADC and Gateway configurations used as SAML identity providers, posing a severe risk to enterprise authentication infrastructure. Security researchers warn that attackers are already probing vulnerable boxes to steal credentials and sensitive application data.

breaking_news.title: Critical Fortinet FortiClient EMS flaw exploited for remote code execution
breaking_news.summary:
Attackers are actively exploiting a critical SQL injection vulnerability in Fortinet's FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) platform. The flaw, identified as CVE-2026-21643, allows unauthenticated threat actors to execute arbitrary code remotely via crafted HTTP requests.

This marks the seventh SQL CVE for Fortinet in the past year, raising concerns about the security posture of the cybersecurity giant's widely deployed management tools. Organizations using FortiClient EMS must apply patches immediately to prevent unauthorized access and potential network compromise.

breaking_news.title: TeamPCP expands supply chain attacks targeting Telnyx and LiteLLM packages
breaking_news.summary:
The TeamPCP hacking group has expanded its supply chain attacks by targeting the Telnyx Python SDK and LiteLLM packages on PyPI. Malicious versions of these packages were uploaded to steal cloud and cryptocurrency credentials from developer environments across multiple operating systems.

This campaign follows a previous breach of the Trivy security tool, demonstrating a coordinated effort to compromise developer infrastructure and security tooling. The group utilizes stealthy payloads, including fake ringtone files, to evade detection while harvesting sensitive access tokens.

breaking_news.title: Iranian hackers breach FBI Director's personal email and publish documents
breaking_news.summary:
Iranian-linked hackers known as Handala breached the personal email account of FBI Director Kash Patel and published his CV and photos online. The FBI confirmed the incident, noting that the compromised information was old, but the State Department offered a $10 million reward for information on the actors.

This high-profile breach highlights the targeting of senior US law enforcement officials by nation-state actors, potentially linked to ongoing geopolitical tensions. The incident underscores the risks to personal digital security even for high-ranking government officials.

breaking_news.title: OpenAI patches critical vulnerabilities in Codex and ChatGPT allowing data exfiltration
breaking_news.summary:
OpenAI patched two critical vulnerabilities in its AI stack, including a flaw in Codex that allowed GitHub token theft via command injection. Another issue in ChatGPT's code execution environment enabled a hidden channel for silently leaking user data through DNS queries.

Researchers warn that giving AI tools autonomy to execute code creates long-term risks, allowing attackers to carry out malicious actions without breaking the model itself. These patches address immediate threats but highlight the need for stricter controls on AI agent interactions with external systems.

breaking_news.title: European Commission confirms data breach after ShinyHunters claims 350GB theft
breaking_news.summary:
The European Commission confirmed a cyberattack on its Europa.eu web platform, with the ShinyHunters extortion group claiming to have stolen over 350GB of data. The breach affected the EU's central online platform hosting websites and services for its institutions.

While the Commission downplayed the impact, the incident represents a significant intrusion into European government infrastructure by a known ransomware and extortion group. Authorities are investigating the scope of the data theft and potential implications for EU digital services.

breaking_news.title: Cisco source code stolen in breach linked to Trivy supply chain attack
breaking_news.summary:
Cisco suffered a cyberattack where threat actors used stolen credentials from the recent Trivy supply chain breach to access its internal development environment. Attackers successfully stole source code belonging to Cisco and its customers, leveraging the initial compromise of the security tool.

This incident demonstrates the cascading risk of supply chain attacks, where a breach in a security tool can lead to compromises of major technology vendors. Cisco is investigating the extent of the data theft and working to secure its development infrastructure against further intrusion.

breaking_news.title: Dark web market lists alleged 375TB of Lockheed Martin data for $600 million
breaking_news.summary:
A dark web market is listing 375TB of alleged Lockheed Martin data for $600 million, with the group claiming responsibility as APT Iran. The massive data dump includes sensitive defense information, though proof of the hack has not been publicly verified yet.

This claim highlights the ongoing threat of nation-state actors targeting critical defense infrastructure and attempting to monetize stolen intellectual property. The sheer volume of data alleged to be stolen underscores the potential impact of successful intrusions into major defense contractors.
